search_provenance: TARI v2 run_002. Real WebSearch via Claude Code WebSearch tool, 2026-05-21. One query per candidate (19 queries total) constructed from cited atoms' verbatim text via build_search_queries.py. Results captured verbatim from WebSearch tool output. Brainstorm seed: deterministic sha256('002').

CAND_002_001.title: Physics of Language Models: Part 2.1, Grade-School Math and the Hidden Reasoning Process
CAND_002_001.snippet: Probing the hidden reasoning process in language models for grade-school math problems. Variant of probing applied to mathematical reasoning chain.

CAND_002_001.title: VAR-MATH: Probing True Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs via Symbolic Multi-Instance Benchmarks
CAND_002_001.snippet: VAR-MATH transforms popular benchmarks like AMC23 and AIME into symbolic variants, showing substantial performance drops for RL-trained models. A variant called V-probing introduces a small trainable update on the input embedding layer for probing properties. Unsolved math reasoning transfer.

CAND_002_001.title: Probing Structural Mathematical Reasoning in Language Models with Algebraic Trapdoors
CAND_002_001.snippet: Probing language model variant performance on mathematical reasoning, focused on unsolved math problems and transfer.

CAND_002_002.title: OpenAI Frontier: Close the Enterprise AI Opportunity Gap
CAND_002_002.snippet: OpenAI has introduced Frontier, a new platform that helps enterprises build, deploy, and manage AI agents that can do real work. Rather than standalone conversational bots, Frontier treats agents less like tools and more like employees. Open problems of frontier model standalone vs composed.

CAND_002_002.title: RE-Bench: Evaluating frontier AI R&D capabilities of language model agents against human experts
CAND_002_002.snippet: Frontier model coding R&D benchmark. AI agents achieving 4x higher than human experts when given a 2-hour budget. Composition with human-AI in coding tasks.

CAND_002_002.title: Open Problems in Frontier AI Risk Management
CAND_002_002.snippet: Open problems in frontier AI risk management. Foundation models in standalone vs composed fashion. NIST Generative AI Profile highlights human-AI interaction.

CAND_002_003.title: Skill Reinforcement Learning and Planning for Open-World Long-Horizon Tasks
CAND_002_003.snippet: Skill RL with skill graphs and bootstrap exploration. Open-world long-horizon RL training. Skillet algorithm for exploration boostrap in RL.

CAND_002_003.title: Enhanced exploration in reinforcement learning using graph neural network based intrinsic reward mechanism
CAND_002_003.snippet: Graph neural network used to find intrinsic exploration rewards in RL. Skill graph used to bootstrap exploration during RL training. Baselines comparison.

CAND_002_003.title: Graph-Based Skill Acquisition For Reinforcement Learning
CAND_002_003.snippet: Skill graph algorithm for RL training. Bootstrap exploration baselines for skill acquisition.

CAND_002_004.title: Population-Evolve: a Parallel Sampling and Evolutionary Method for LLM Math Reasoning
CAND_002_004.snippet: Parallel sampling for LLM mathematical reasoning. Exploration beyond simple parallel sampling. Open problem in machine learning mathematics.

CAND_002_004.title: The Road Less Traveled: Enhancing Exploration in LLMs via Sequential Sampling
CAND_002_004.snippet: Lack of exploration plays a role in the performance gap between parallel and sequential sampling in large reasoning models. Open problem in ML proof generation.

CAND_002_004.title: Understanding Performance Gap Between Parallel and Sequential Sampling in Large Reasoning Models
CAND_002_004.snippet: As the RL policy converges to a high-reward solution, the outputs become increasingly similar across different samples. Exploration beyond parallel sampling.

CAND_002_005.title: Unearthing Skill-Level Insights for Understanding Trade-Offs of Foundation Models
CAND_002_005.snippet: Skill-based framework for foundation model evaluation. Skill-specific analysis on benchmarks. ML Gym skill algorithm baselines comparison composition.

CAND_002_005.title: SkillsBench: Benchmarking How Well Agent Skills Work Across Diverse Tasks
CAND_002_005.snippet: Skill composition benchmark for foundation models. Performance profile across diverse tasks. Standalone fashion comparison.

CAND_002_005.title: Guiding Skill Discovery with Foundation Models
CAND_002_005.snippet: Skill discovery with foundation models. Algorithm composition and benchmark performance across science domains.

CAND_002_006.title: Test-Time Adaptation by Causal Trimming
CAND_002_006.snippet: Test-time adaptation reducing non-causal features for more reliable predictions. Structural causal model applied to test-time training. Help model learn exploration during test time.

CAND_002_006.title: Test-Time Learning of Causal Structure from Interventional Data
CAND_002_006.snippet: TICL test-time interventional causal learning addresses generalization. Structural causal model with test-time training paradigm.

CAND_002_006.title: Test-Time Training Provably Improves Transformers as In-Context Learners
CAND_002_006.snippet: Test-time training provably helps transformers learn. Exploration at test time vs training time. Causal structure adaptation.

CAND_002_007.title: Foundation Models for Knowledge Graph Reasoning
CAND_002_007.snippet: ULTRA foundation models for knowledge graph reasoning. Skill graph algorithm with baselines. Math reasoning open problem.

CAND_002_007.title: MathVista: Evaluating Mathematical Reasoning of Foundation Models in Visual Contexts
CAND_002_007.snippet: Mathematical reasoning benchmark for foundation models. Skill graph and algorithm composition with math reasoning open problem.

CAND_002_007.title: Evaluating Large Language Models on Solved and Unsolved Problems in Graph Theory
CAND_002_007.snippet: For open problems, LLMs generate coherent exploratory reasoning but do not produce new mathematical insight. Open problem ML graph theory composition.

CAND_002_008.title: Learning to Discover at Test Time
CAND_002_008.snippet: Test-time training approaches to discover solutions. TTT-Discover combines exploration with reward-based state selection. Token sampling exploration during inference.

CAND_002_008.title: Test-time Prompt Intervention
CAND_002_008.snippet: Test-time prompt intervention dynamically guides reasoning paths during inference through interventions and post-intervention sampling. Human-AI collaboration in coding tasks.

CAND_002_008.title: ChatCollab: Exploring Collaboration Between Humans and AI Agents in Software Teams
CAND_002_008.snippet: Multi-user human-AI collaboration in coding tasks. Test-time token sampling for collaborative discovery. Frontier model coding announcements.

CAND_002_009.title: Training Language Models to Explain Their Own Computations
CAND_002_009.snippet: Privileged Access Hypothesis: models trained to explain their own internal mechanisms do so more accurately than models trained to explain others. Self-explanation generalization to frontier coding.

CAND_002_009.title: Privileged Information Distillation for Language Models
CAND_002_009.snippet: Privileged information at training time enables language models to succeed on tasks they would otherwise fail. Transfer to frontier coding without privileged info at inference.

CAND_002_009.title: Self-Interpretability: LLMs Can Describe Complex Internal Processes that Drive Their Decisions
CAND_002_009.snippet: Contemporary LLMs are capable of providing accurate, quantitative descriptions of their own internal processes. Training generalizes to other complex decisions.

CAND_002_010.title: The Secret Behind Fast LLM Inference: Unlocking the KV Cache
CAND_002_010.snippet: KV Cache changes computational cost from quadratic to linear, dramatically lowering latency. 7x speedup over single forward pass. Latent world model fast inference.

CAND_002_010.title: KV-Latent: Dimensional-level KV Cache Reduction with Frequency-aware Rotary Positional Embedding
CAND_002_010.snippet: Latent KV cache reduction. Transformer fast inference via dimensional compression. Up to 26x throughput than standard transformers.

CAND_002_010.title: Multi-Head Latent Attention KV Cache Optimization
CAND_002_010.snippet: Multi-head Latent Attention compresses KV tensors into a shared latent space. Transformer fast inference speed for language models. Latent world models tracking state.

CAND_002_011.title: Training Language Models to Explain Their Own Computations
CAND_002_011.snippet: When fine-tuned for self-explanation, a model produces more accurate descriptions of its own internal states than an external model. Performance profile of self-description. Train models to explain.

CAND_002_011.title: Self-Interpretability: LLMs Can Describe Complex Internal Processes that Drive Their Decisions
CAND_002_011.snippet: Models can provide accurate quantitative descriptions of their own internal processes. Performance profile after self-explanation training.

CAND_002_011.title: Unearthing Skill-Level Insights for Understanding Trade-Offs of Foundation Models
CAND_002_011.snippet: Skill-based foundation model evaluation. Performance profile baselines for self-description training. ML Gym with performance profiles.

CAND_002_012.title: Frontier LLM vs Open-Source: How do you actually decide?
CAND_002_012.snippet: Frontier vs open-source standalone model comparison. Open problems in frontier model selection. Standalone vs composition with human-AI.

CAND_002_012.title: FrontierSmith: Synthesizing Open-Ended Coding Problems at Scale
CAND_002_012.snippet: Open problem coding tasks for frontier models. Contrast with traditional benchmarks. Standalone fashion vs collaboration model.

CAND_002_012.title: Frontier Model Benchmark Comparison March 2026
CAND_002_012.snippet: Comparative benchmark of frontier models. Standalone fashion comparison. Frontier model open problem contrast.

CAND_002_013.title: RLVE: Scaling Up Reinforcement Learning for Language Models with Adaptive Verifiable Environments
CAND_002_013.snippet: Predefines difficulty levels over an infinite problem set from each verifiable environment, enabling training on infinite problem sets for RL-based language models. Effective algorithm to scale test-time adaptation infinitely.

CAND_002_013.title: Reinforcement Learning Teachers of Test Time Scaling
CAND_002_013.snippet: RLT trained specifically to yield effective downstream distillation for test-time scaling. Skill algorithm scale test-time adaptation infinitely.

CAND_002_013.title: TTRL: Test-Time Reinforcement Learning
CAND_002_013.snippet: Test-time RL with infinite scaling behavior. Algorithm trained to scale test-time adaptation. Goal of effective algorithm scaling.

CAND_002_014.title: Test-Time Model Merging (TTMM)
CAND_002_014.snippet: Test-time model merging is a family of inference-time algorithms for integrating multiple specialized neural models into a single, unified model without retraining. Multi-user version of test-time training.

CAND_002_014.title: Local Mixtures of Experts: Essentially Free Test-Time Training via Model Merging
CAND_002_014.snippet: Local mixtures of experts for test-time training via model merging. Shared model across multiple users learning algorithm.

CAND_002_014.title: TTT4Rec: A Test-Time Training Approach for Rapid Adaption in Sequential Recommendation
CAND_002_014.snippet: Multi-user test-time training in recommendation contexts. Algorithm learns from user interactions during testing. Shared model across users.

CAND_002_015.title: Unearthing Skill-Level Insights for Understanding Trade-Offs of Foundation Models
CAND_002_015.snippet: Skill-based foundation model evaluation with performance profiles. ML Gym deployment at Meta. Standalone model composition with human-AI for science.

CAND_002_015.title: Human-Centric Evaluation for Foundation Models
CAND_002_015.snippet: Performance profile for foundation model science benchmark with human-AI standalone composition.

CAND_002_015.title: Foundation Model Evaluation Benchmark
CAND_002_015.snippet: Systematically designed protocol for foundation model evaluation including performance profiles and standalone vs composition modes.

CAND_002_016.title: Counterfactual Simulation Training for Chain-of-Thought Faithfulness
CAND_002_016.snippet: CST rewards CoTs that enable a simulator to accurately predict a model's outputs over counterfactual inputs. Janet gave Jack the book counterfactual reasoning trained at test time.

CAND_002_016.title: Reasoning or Reciting? Exploring the Capabilities and Limitations of Language Models Through Counterfactual Tasks
CAND_002_016.snippet: Counterfactual task variants challenge language model reasoning. Janet/Jack book possession kind of counterfactual probe. Train algorithm to adapt at test time.

CAND_002_016.title: Evidence from counterfactual tasks supports emergent analogical reasoning in LLMs
CAND_002_016.snippet: Counterfactual tasks reveal emergent analogical reasoning. Effectively trained algorithm to match bigger model performance. Counterfactual input analogies.

CAND_002_017.title: KernelBench: Can LLMs Write GPU Kernels?
CAND_002_017.snippet: KernelBench benchmark framework with 250 PyTorch workloads to evaluate LLM-generated GPU kernels. ML Gym performance profiles. GPU kernel engineering example.

CAND_002_017.title: AgentKernelArena: Generalization-Aware Benchmarking of GPU Kernel Optimization Agents
CAND_002_017.snippet: GPU kernel optimization agent benchmark with 196 tasks. Performance profile evaluation for kernel engineering ML agent.

CAND_002_017.title: AutoKernel: Autonomous GPU Kernel Optimization via Iterative Agent-Driven Search
CAND_002_017.snippet: Autonomous agent loop for GPU kernel optimization with performance profiling and benchmark comparison. ML Gym style kernel engineering example.

CAND_002_018.title: DeepSWE: Training a Fully Open-sourced, State-of-the-Art Coding Agent by Scaling RL
CAND_002_018.snippet: Reasoning-enabled coding agent trained with RL. Successful test execution yields positive reward while test failures yield zero terminal reward. Composition with human-AI in coding.

CAND_002_018.title: Learning on the Job: Test-Time Curricula for Targeted Reinforcement Learning
CAND_002_018.snippet: Test-time curricula RL with terminal reward. Improved reasoning on target tasks. Composition of human-AI for coding RL. Terminal reward final answer correctness.

CAND_002_018.title: Learning to Discover at Test Time
CAND_002_018.snippet: TTT-Discover combines exploration bonuses with reward-based state selection. Terminal reward final answer for test-time training.

CAND_002_019.title: Transformers as Support Vector Machines
CAND_002_019.snippet: Formal equivalence between self-attention optimization geometry and SVM problems separating optimal input tokens. Transformer attention layer token representations. Open problem of math reasoning.

CAND_002_019.title: Enhancing the Transformer with Explicit Relational Encoding for Math Problem Solving
CAND_002_019.snippet: Transformer attention with explicit relational encoding for math reasoning open problem. Token vector representations across layers attending to prior tokens.

CAND_002_019.title: The emergence of clusters in self-attention dynamics
CAND_002_019.snippet: Token representations through transformer layers cluster via self-attention dynamics. Math open problem of token attention layer-by-layer representations.
